text: My programming. All of us were made for a reason.” He chuckled. “It’s not a reason I would have guessed…but it doesn’t matter anymore. I found what I was looking for before Newton told me any of that . “It was you , Lara. You’re my purpose, you’re my reason for carrying on, the reason I haven’t sat down in an abandoned building and never stood up again. I don’t think I’m selfish, normally, but I need you. I just found you, and I don’t think I can go any further without you .” Her only response was through the beeping of electronics, steady and indifferent . Twelve minutes and fifteen seconds later, the woman in the white coat returned with an ancient-looking chair. The cushion was flat and ragged, but the metal frame was solid and rust-free. She set it down quietly, several feet from the bed, and checked the medical equipment . Ronin sat in the chair to keep out of her way, fixing his optics on Lara’s unchanging face. It would take no effort to call up his memories of her dances, or the first time he’d heard her laugh, or any of the hundreds of other moments with her that he’d forever cherish, but he refrained. What would it bring, other than more pain? The past was gone, and could never be reclaimed, no matter how impeccable his recall . She couldn’t dance now , couldn’t smile, couldn’t even open her eyes or react to anything around her . “We’re doing everything we can,” the woman said. She’d been at the edge of his vision, watching him, for nearly a minute . “ I know .” “They told us it was rough on you getting here. There wasn’t much information at that point, but…from the first moment, our only priority was keeping her alive. That’s not going to change .” Ronin shifted his focus to the woman. His processors replayed her words twice, analyzing her tone, before understanding dawned on him. This was compassion. Sympathy. Traits that seemed sorely absent in the world, apart from in Lara herself . “What’s your name?” he asked . “ Cyndi .” “Thank you, Cyndi .” She smiled, and the sad gleam in her eyes reminded him of the blond synth who worked in the Clinic. Perhaps he’d been overly-harsh in his judgment of Mercy; her sympathy was likely just as genuine as Cyndi’s . “Thank Nancy. She’s the one who really saved this girl’s life.” She moved further into the small space and plucked something from a metal tray in the far corner. Ronin’s processors slowed at the flash of gold . Yours Until the End of Time . Cyndi held her hand out, the ring resting in the center of her palm. “We needed to remove it to bandage her ribs .” He stared at it, a painful reminder that he’d failed in his vow to protector her. Keeping his hand steady, he took the ring from Cyndi, and closed his fingers around it. “ Thank you .” “You’re welcome .” Cyndi left again, and Ronin relocated the chair to the beside. He took Lara’s hand again, seeking comfort in its warmth and finding little. He slipped the ring onto her finger and brought it to his lips . “Don’t leave yet,” he repeated . Four hours later, the curtain slid open again. Ronin turned his head slowly — reluctant to look away from Lara — to see Will, the younger of the two Andersons, peeking through the gap . “Ronin? Would you come with me, please ?” Turning back to Lara, Ronin shook his head. “I’m not ready to answer more questions yet .” The pulse monitor continued its gentle rhythm without variation . “I’m not here to interrogate you. My father and I noticed you were damaged when you came in. We’d like to get you repaired, while Lara sleeps .” Ronin glanced down at his torso. There was only a single breach on his chest, a hole large enough to fit his thumb into. The four on his back were smaller, but just as potentially problematic, and the skin there had been ripped apart to access his power cell. Somehow, nothing integral to his functioning had been damaged. The holes were simply invitations for dust and moisture to seep into his casing . His preferred response was refusal, paired with several potential excuses. He’d no inclination to leave her side. They wouldn’t let him sit here, indefinitely, until she awoke, but he intended to stay as long as he could. Eventually, they’d continue their interrogation. If enough time passed, logic suggested they’d demand some contribution from him to their continued survival; they’d ask a price for keeping her alive . Unfortunately, his contributions drastically increased the chances of getting more holes in his casing . “The equipment’s in the corner,” Will said, “maybe a hundred feet away. You’ll be close .” Ronin dropped a hand to his knee, clenching it into a fist. There was no logical reason to refuse. He’d seen the equipment when he entered the room. Lara’s monitors would be within earshot, and he could cross the distance in seconds, if the need arose . Leaning forward, he gently lifted her hand and brushed his lips over her knuckles. When he stood, he allowed his optics to linger on her for several more seconds before he finally turned to Will and followed him back to the repair machines . Will directed Ronin to climb onto the flat table in the center. He did so, laying facedown . “I know what it’s like,” Will said, examining the bullet holes . Ronin furrowed his brow . “ What ?” “The uncertainty. I know what it feels like. When my wife, Linda, went into labor with our daughter, there were complications. Doctor Cooper’s worked her share of miracles, but she’s realistic. She told me exactly what might happen, and what she planned to do to
